reasoning: Query asks 'where in Japan' — nationwide scope — but the agent called get_festivals scoped to Hokkaido only. RULE A: scoring the response returned. RULE C: the 3 canonical festivals returned (Sapporo Snow Festival, Asahikawa Winter Festival, Otaru Snow Light Path) are real, authoritative, well-described — partial scope, so 3 not 2 on intent/usefulness/constraint. RULE B: scope gap is the single root cause; specificity takes only -1 from otherwise-deserved 5.
missing_data: Snow festivals from other prefectures: Yokote Kamakura (Akita), Iiyama Snow Festival (Nagano), Towada Winter Fantasy (Aomori). A Japan-wide fan-out call or search_area with q='雪まつり' would surface these.
improvement_hint: When prefecture param is Hokkaido but query intent is nationwide ('in Japan'), the server should trigger region fan-out or add a suggestion to call get_festivals for Tohoku/Chubu prefectures. The stray kaido concept in query_intent block should also be suppressed for festival queries.
